---
title: 'News... Dealer Services'
date: '2014-04-22T19:11:00.0030000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: '(My Journal) Fed By Faith'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '6300140621901811532'
blogger_filename: '/2014/04/news-dealer-services.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/(My Journal) Fed By Faith/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Journal'
---

<h4>
News</h4>
Ed Reiss (My Supervisor at&nbsp;Wells Fargo&nbsp;Inbound Sales) pulled me aside as I was headed to lunch. He got a call today asking about me and filled out a form the other day. He said he can't make ANY promises, so not to hold him to over promising, but he's never got a call like this where the job wasn't eventually offered, and he's never made a call like this&nbsp;himself without offering the job.&nbsp;He only makes these calls to remove all doubt before proceeding with a&nbsp;decision that is basically made already.<br />
<br />
She said&nbsp;she's&nbsp;going to try to wrap up decisions today (which means offers, if any, will be made this week.)<br />
<br />
I believe God can do the supernatural and get me an offer at or near midpoint on the pay scale.&nbsp;God, I'm asking for the highest pay available to offer. You can take care of the rest.<br />
<br />
******<br />
<br />
Seperate but related topic...<br />
<br />
*****<br />
<br />
I've been listening to <a href="http://www.oneplace.com/ministries/the-alternative/listen/your-passion-your-purpose-399453.html" target="_blank">Tony Evans</a> today.<br />
<br />
Your Passion &amp; Your PurposeCalled for a Purpose03/31/2014<br />
<br />
<br />
Your Passion &amp; Your PurposeCalled for a Purpose03/30/2014<br />
<br />
&nbsp;<a href="http://www.oneplace.com/ministries/the-alternative/listen/">http://www.oneplace.com/ministries/the-alternative/listen/</a> 
&nbsp; 
And the Steward Series in April. 
&nbsp; 
&nbsp; 
&nbsp; 
He talked about: 
&nbsp; 
If you always want to get a new job but not share Christ with others that's materialism. 
&nbsp; 
<blockquote class="tr_bq">
Whenever you want more&nbsp;FROM God but there's not more FOR God you have mixed priorities. </blockquote>
&nbsp; 
Passion is a fire inside of you. It's something you can't not do. It's something you think about when you are doing other things, and it's something that keeps you up at night. It's something you do even though you're not being paid for it. 
&nbsp; 
Your calling is something that aligns your passion and your aptitude. 
&nbsp; 
If you have passion AND your good at it AND you can produce for others with it, you have calling. 
&nbsp; 
Good To Great says the three circles are 
&nbsp; 
"Passion": What you absolutely LOVE to do! 
&nbsp; 
"Best In The World": Genetic or God Given Talent! 
&nbsp; 
"Economic Engine": Well Paid for what you do. 
&nbsp; 
&nbsp; 
<div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;">
<a href="/images/imported/www-rookie-manager-com/hedgehog-v1-2-aec34cc269fa.jpg" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" closure_lm_537763="null" src="/images/imported/www-rookie-manager-com/hedgehog-v1-2-aec34cc269fa.jpg" uta="true" /></a></div>
&nbsp; 
&nbsp; 
I'd like to resolve this with a Kingdom Clarification. 
&nbsp; 
Economic Engine only describes the fruit, not the root. People PAY for something that solves a problem, enhances their experience, or adds value to them. 
&nbsp; 
&nbsp; 
The Five Fold "Ministry" is there to train the PEOPLE OF GOD to minister OUTSIDE the Church walls. 
&nbsp; 
Therefore my Ministry is OUT THERE in the marketplace. Even if I did go to work for the "Church" my Ministry would be those things I do OUTSIDE the Church walls. All I do inside the Church is training for ministry. 
&nbsp; 
<blockquote class="tr_bq">
"Whever your&nbsp;financial life is more important than your spiritual life you are living a herasy." Tony Evans. </blockquote>
&nbsp; 
&nbsp; 
Going back to work now. 
&nbsp; 
DW 
&nbsp; 
&nbsp; 
&nbsp; 
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
